important_concepts.label: When should storage move beyond JSON?

provenance.excerpt: Use a graph of concepts and relationships.
Track where every claim came from.
Sensor logs are evidence: timestamped, source-attributed readings.
Garden sensors: a small ESP32 build for the greenhouse.
The greenhouse build is the first newsletter arc.
The sensor project consumed the whole quarter.
Keep owner data local unless explicitly exported.
The deep-sleep guide documents real-world power draw.
The local-first paper defines the storage principles.
The digital-garden essay argues for evolving notes.
The GraphRAG survey maps the retrieval landscape.
CogGRAG is a public reference for graph retrieval.
The greenhouse should mostly look after itself.
A handful of nodes reporting over one protocol.
Hard power budgets change every design choice.
Sensor readings could enter the graph as provenance-backed observations.
A sensor log line is an excerpt about the world.
Writing about systems clarifies them.
Manual review is the bottleneck; when does ingest become automatic?
Cold weather halves capacity; winter is the real test.
One issue a month, no heroics.
Let soil moisture drive the drip valve directly.
Every project doubles as newsletter material.
Writing pressure kept the sensor build honest.
Build a local-first toolkit for source-backed AI memory.
Projects should end finished, not abandoned.

proof_trail.label: When should storage move beyond JSON?

provenance.excerpt: When should storage move beyond JSON?
Make assistant responses easier to verify and continue.
Deep-sleep nodes might run for months on small cells.
Boring tech unless the project is the experiment.
